title: onobrychis sulphurea subsp. pallida
intro: Onobrychis sulphurea subsp. pallida, also known as a subspecies of the yellow clover or dyer's clover, is a plant species within the pea family Fabaceae. Onobrychis sulphurea subsp. pallida has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other secondary metabolites. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting cardiovascular health. Onobrychis sulphurea subsp. pallida can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues.
